keyPoints: Expert diagnosis involves aggregating advice from multiple experts to make a decision.
The goal is to perform nearly as well as the best expert in hindsight.
Algorithms like the Weighted Majority Algorithm are used to minimize regret over time.
commonMistakes: Assuming the best expert is known ahead of time, when the algorithm must learn it.
Confusing expert diagnosis with traditional game theory where all players are strategic.
Forgetting that the cost of not following the best expert is called 'regret'.
